prompt: Look into IDEA.md and understand what I want to build. There's the response from an AI tool as well, but do not value it too much, just some input for you. My aim is to build a minimal friction tool. We can start with just codex support as the MVP. But it must work across the CLI app as well as the desktop app of codex.

Let's continue defining what we are building. I am wondering, are the logs we produce include information such as
- ai model being used
- tokens consumed
- latency
- tool usage (which tools were invoked by ai)
- estimated $ spent
Ok let's pivot to using the OTel layer as our source of data. We can estimate the $ cost ourselves using the pricing data from codex
Yes please, let's move on with TypeScript, we need that type safety and node is good for npm first developer tools
Yes please, let's normalize what we can also do the fixes you suggested.

Ok, please maintain a customer facing readme file, I think some of these details should go in there as well. Like recommended usage. Let's assume we are working towards the first release, so docs should be kept up to date always. Also please consider time to time to update AGENTS.md to document the patterns we like to apply in this repo.

Ok let's move to monorepo and other parts of the product. We can start with a simple presentation layer. I want a CLI command that will process the data we collect and let me know these per the repo it worked on:

1. % of AI generated code vs user code
2. Total tokens consumed
3. A histogram of AI activity per day
4. Costs
5. Total time spent by AI
6. Tool invocations

The cost calculation can use openai's pricing page data https://developers.openai.com/codex/pricing and if you need to check what tokens are and how to calculate it https://help.openai.com/en/articles/4936856-what-are-tokens-and-how-to-count-them

Since we are still in development and have no customers except myself we don't need backwards compatibility. That is, do not worry about already generated data. Just wipe it and start afresh if needed.
Let's rename the tool to eyes4ai, both the project as well as the cli.
Let's rename the directory as well
Let's change the storage directory choice of ".ai" as ".eyes4ai"

Let's simplify README.md it has too much clutter and it is too robotic. As a customer, I want to see at a glance:
- how it works
- what data it sources and from where
- how it stores the data locally, what is the schema it uses when storing the data
Please go over all the links in the docs and ensure they use relative paths. I tried the docs/schema.md link in the readme.md file and it is broken because it refers to full path instead of relative.
